{{Has quick guide}}
{{Infobox Quest
|name = Tale of the Righteous
|number = 137
|image = [[File:Tale of the Righteous.png|300px]]
|release = [[19 April]] [[2018]]
|update = Tale of the Righteous and Kourend Favour
|members = Yes
|series = [[Quests/Series#Kourend|Great Kourend]], #3E
|developer = [[Mod Ed]], [[Mod Kieren]]
}}
'''Tale of the Righteous''' is a [[quest]] announced on 21 March 2018{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ed|url=https://twitter.com/JagexEd/status/976455666406711296|date=21 March 2018|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/100521|archivedate=29 May 2020|quote=For those of you who've been asking about Kourend quests, here's something I've worked on with @JagexKieren that you might like.}} and polled in [[Poll:Old School Content Poll 58: More QoL and a Quest|Old School Content Poll #58]], which it passed. It follows [[Client of Kourend]] in the [[Great Kourend quest series]].

The quest can be started by speaking with [[Phileas Rimor]] in his house just west of the [[The Cloak and Stagger]] tavern in [[Shayzien]].

==Details==
{{Quest details
|start = Speak to [[Phileas Rimor]] in his house west of [[The Cloak and Stagger]] tavern in [[Shayzien]].
|startmap = 1542,3570
|difficulty = Intermediate
|description = Over a thousand years ago, King Shayzien VII ordered an expedition to Mount Quidamortem. The expedition never returned and it was assumed that all members perished in the field. However, a recent discovery suggests this may not have been the case. Phileas Rimor is the descendant of the expedition leader and he is desperate to discover the truth. Join him and delve into a conspiracy a thousand years in the making in "Tale of the Righteous".
|length = Short
|requirements = *Completion of the following quests:
**[[Client of Kourend]]
***[[X Marks the Spot]]
*{{SCP|Strength|16|link=yes}} {{Boostable|no}} {{Questreqstart|yes}}
*{{SCP|Mining|10|link=yes}} {{Boostable|no}} {{Questreqstart|yes}}
|items = *A [[pickaxe]]
*A [[ranged]] weapon with ammunition. Darts and throwing knives also work.
*Runes for 2-3 casts of any [[magic]] [[combat spell]]
*A [[melee]] weapon of any kind ([[pickaxe]] will work)
*[[Rope]]
|recommended = *{{SCP|Combat|20|link=yes}}
*[[Stamina potion]] and [[weight-reducing clothing]]
*Any kind of [[antipoison]] or [[Shayzien armour (tier 5)]] as you will encounter various types of poison dealing [[Lizardman]], including the final boss.
*[[Food]] for low level players without a shortcut to the top of [[Mount Quidamortem]]
* 20 [[coins]] to use the [[Lovakengj Minecart Network]] and travel to [[Mount Quidamortem]] (free if [[The Forsaken Tower]] is completed)
*A charged [[Xeric's talisman]] for teleporting to [[Mount Quidamortem]] (requires unlocking it with an [[Ancient tablet]]) and [[Xeric's Lookout]].
*Ability to teleport to [[Arceuus Library]].
|kills = [[Corrupt Lizardman]] (level 46)
|leagueRegion = <poem>
Unlocking {{LeagueRegion|Kourend}} {{okay}} will auto-complete the quest
</poem>
}}

==Walkthrough==
===Getting started===
[[File:Phileas Rimor chathead.png|left]]
Speak to [[Phileas Rimor]] in his house just west of the [[The Cloak and Stagger]] tavern in [[Shayzien]]. ([[Xeric's talisman|Xeric's Talisman]] to Xeric's Lookout or fairy ring {{Fairycode|DJR}}) Ask him if he needs any help, and he will tell you that his ancestor, Magnus Rimor, led an expedition to [[Mount Quidamortem]] in the [[Kebos Lowlands]] over a thousand years ago, and never returned, with the records declaring he perished along with the party.
[[File:Tale of the Righteous - talking to Phileas.png|thumb|270px|A player talks to Phileas in his home.]]
However, a few days ago, he received a parcel from an unknown individual containing an [[Shayzien journal|old journal]] written by King Shayzien VII, with a note on the parcel claiming the journal was stolen from the [[Tower of Magic]] during the uprising against [[Xeric]] in the 47th decade. Although most of the journal is illegible, he managed to find an extract stating "''Quidamortem's creatures are compatible. The experimentation is proving successful and we will soon be ready to mass produce.''"

The extract had a date in the 37th decade, and yet the expedition was declared a failure a decade before, leading Phileas to question why the king wrote about Quidamortem a decade after the failure of the expedition. He will ask you to discover the truth about his ancestor's expedition; agree to help him. He will ask you to try and learn more about the expedition by visiting the [[Library Historical Archive]].

===Perhaps the archives are incomplete===
{{Needed|A [[melee]] weapon, a [[ranged]] weapon with ammunition (darts and throwing knives also work), and runes to cast any [[magic]] [[combat spell]]. '''Note:''' Bring enough [[runes]] for more than one cast - it's possible to "fail" the attack spell. A [[swamp lizard]] or equivalent may be used; just swap between each combat style.}}

Go to the [[Arceuus Library]], and teleport to the Archive via [[Archeio]] on the {{FloorNumber|uk=0}}. Speak to [[Pagida]], who has been investigating King Shayzien VII's additions to the [[Tower of Magic]].

Speak to [[Pagida]], and he will tell you that King Shayzien VII spent more time in the Tower of Magic than any other King, and oversaw construction of various new sections, including a prison, which was built around the same time as the expedition, but has been abandoned for many years. You will ask if you can access the prison, and he will agree to do so.

[[File:Tale of the Righteous - cleansing crystals.png|thumb|right|Cleansing the crystals in the Tower of Magic's prison.]]
The prison contains a strange device in the centre that can be pushed towards the north, east, south and west. Players can obtain a [[dusty note]] (optional) by searching the skeleton to the south-west part of the prison. The note provides clues of the objective, which is to have all the crystals cleansed in order to open the north prison door:

*Push the device as far west as you can, then stand on the north side of the device and attack the device using magic.
**'''Note: Attacking the [[Strange Device (Tale of the Righteous)|Strange Device]] with [[ice spells]] will result in the player being unable to push the device for the duration of the freeze effect.'''
*Then, standing on the south side of the device, attack it using melee (your fists do not work, you need a melee weapon equipped).
**'''Note: If this doesn't work, make sure your NPC Attack Options are not hidden'''
*Push the device all the way east, and attack the device using ranged from its south side.

'''Note: You must bring all 3 attack styles with you at the same time. If you have to leave this area only partially completing it, you will have to redo this segment again.'''

Once all the crystals have been cleansed, the prison doors to the north will open. Investigate the skeleton inside, and the player will read some writings carved into the floor stating, "''Lizards on the mountain. Do not trust the King. The altar is a lie."''

===Another expedition===
{{Needed|A [[pickaxe]] and [[rope]]. [[Equipment]] and [[food]] to defeat a level 46 enemy. '''Note:''' Bring an [[antipoison]] for your fight with the corrupt lizardman. 20 coins if using the minecart to Mount Quidamortem.}}

Return to [[Phileas]], and tell him of your discovery. He will ask that you attempt the same expedition in order to find out what the original team discovered, and asks you to speak to [[Lord Shiro Shayzien]] in the [[Shayzien Encampment]].

[[File:Path to Raids.png|thumb|280px|Getting to Mount Quidamortem from the Shayziens' Wall.]]
Make your way to the [[War Tent]] within the encampment, and climb the ladder inside to reach Lord Shayzien on the {{FloorNumber|uk=1}}. You will ask him if you can go on an expedition to Mount Quidamortem. He will agree on the condition that any discoveries made are to be brought to him first, as King Shayzien VII was his ancestor and he wants to be made aware of any discoveries that may present him in a bad light.

Head to the top of [[Mount Quidamortem]]. You can use fairy ring {{Fairycode|BLS}} or a minecart to get closer. You should bring food and some type of [[antipoison]] as you will run past various aggressive, poison-inflicting [[lizardmen]] on the trip to the mountain regardless of your player level. 

'''Tip''': Talk to the [[mountain guide]] at the peak to unlock the ability to quick travel to and from the [[Shayziens' Wall]].

After you arrive at the peak, speak to [[Historian Duffy]] in the tent to the east (the water symbol on the map). You will tell him of the dusty note you found in the prison, leading him to believe that the lizardmen were not of natural origin and have existed before [[Xeric]] did.

He will tell you that a crevice to the west of the mountain was recently uncovered, and that it may lead to what you are searching for. Make your way to the west of the mountain, and use your rope on the crevice. Enter the crevice to find yourself in a [[Quidamortem Cave|red cave]]. Mine the first boulder with a pickaxe, and push the second boulder.

[[File:Tale of the Righteous - fighting Corrupt Lizardman.png|thumb|left|250px|Fighting the corrupt lizardman.]]
[[File:Tale of the Righteous - inspecting Unstable Altar.png|thumb|250px|A player inspects the unstable altar, and hears a mysterious voice.]]
Upon walking close to the magic gate, a [[Corrupt Lizardman]] will appear and attack you. '''The corrupt lizardman will deal poison like the other lizardmen, so an antipoison will be useful. If you die at this battle your grave appears outside of the crevice.''' Defeat it, and inspect the [[Unstable Altar]] past the gate. You will hear a [[Mysterious Voice]] exclaiming "''Rickard! Turn away! Don't do this!''", but you will be unable to make sense of it.

Return to Duffy and tell him of your discoveries in the [[Quidamortem Cave|cave]]. The museum expedition will then travel into the cave; follow them. Once inside the cave, speak to Duffy. He will tell you the cave does indeed predate Xeric, and wonders if it was King Shayzien VII who created the lizardmen. He will add that some believe the two are one and the same. He will then tell you to speak to [[Gnosi]].
[[File:Tale of the Righteous - cave temple.png|thumb|250px|The temple housing an unstable altar.]]
Speak to Gnosi who is nearby. She will tell you the reason Xeric ruled Great Kourend unopposed for over a century was because of the power of the [[Dark Altar]], and that he came to Mount Quidamortem to construct the [[Chambers of Xeric]] after his exile. Gnosi believes the reason Xeric survived in Quidamortem is the unstable altar nearby, which appears to give off the same power as the Dark Altar. She further states that it was created to amplify the Dark Altar's power, allowing it to reach areas it previously could not. As it is still giving out power, Gnosi wonders if Xeric had used it to extend his lifespan. She will then tell you to report these findings to the [[Arceuus Library|Library]], but you tell her that Lord Shayzien asked you to report to him first should any discoveries be made.

===Finishing up===
[[File:Tale of the Righteous - Phileas house trashed.png|thumb|right|Phileas' house has been trashed, and he has mysteriously disappeared!]]
Return to [[Lord Shiro Shayzien]] (talk to the [[Mountain Guide]] to make the journey faster if you don't have a teleport or the [[Lovakengj Minecart Network]]) and inform him of your discovery. He will ask for proof, and asks you to visit [[Phileas]] for the journal. Return to Phileas, only to find that his house has been trashed and he himself has gone missing.

Return to Lord Shayzien once more, and he will open an investigation into Phileas' disappearance. Without the parcel, no concrete evidence can be linked to King Shayzien VII and your discoveries, but he will contact the research team and ensure their discoveries are documented.

'''Congratulations, quest complete!'''

==Rewards==
{{Quest rewards
|name = Tale of the Righteous
|image = [[File:Tale of the Righteous reward scroll.png|center]]
|qp = 1
|rewards =
*8,000 [[coins]]
*A page for [[Kharedst's memoirs]], '[[History and hearsay]].' It teleports players to the [[Graveyard of Heroes]] south-west of the Shayzien bank.
*Ability to recolour the [[graceful outfit]] to the colours of [[Shayzien]].
*Ability to fight the [[Corrupt Lizardman]] within the [[Nightmare Zone]].
*3 [[Xerician fabric]] (dropped by Corrupt Lizardman)
}}

==Required for completing==
Completion of Tale of the Righteous is required for the following:
*[[A Kingdom Divided]]
*[[Kourend & Kebos Diary#Medium|Kourend & Kebos Medium Diary]]

==Transcript==
{{hastranscript|Quest}}

==Changes==
{{Subject changes header}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 10 January 2024
|update = Children of the Sun
|poll = Varlamore Activities & Kourend Changes
|change = 
* As [[Kourend Favour]] has been removed, the quest no longer requires it to start. Additionally, the [[Shayzien favour certificate]] is no longer rewarded.
* Completion of the quest now unlocks the ability to recolour a [[graceful outfit]] to the colours of [[Shayzien]].
}}
{{Subject changes footer}}

==Trivia==
[[File:Library Historical Archive - old jail cells.png|thumb|right|The appearance of the Tower of Magic's prison prior to the release of the Tale of the Righteous.]]
*Prior to the release of the quest, the prison in the [[Tower of Magic]] that players access during the quest had a different appearance, with the strange device in the centre having a coffin instead.
*Prior to the release of [[A Kingdom Divided]], when Pagida asked the player if they would like to hear about the [[Tower of Magic]], players gave a response depending on their status, which has since been removed from the game.
**Regular players responded saying ''"Well it's either this or buying rubbish at the Grand Exchange, fire away."''
**[[Ironman]] players will instead reply ''"Well it's either that or running around telling everyone I'm an Ironman."'' This is a reference to the common joke that Ironman players have a need to tell any player they meet that they are an Ironman.
*In the prison, examining the skeleton in the western cell will result in the message "Must have been a [[Hardcore ironman|Hardcore Ironman]]."
*In the prison, examining the [[Strange Bells]] in the south-eastern corner will result in the message "For whom?". This might be a reference to [https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Meditation_XVII a 1623 text] by {{WP|John Donne}},  {{WP|For Whom The Bell Tolls|the 1940 novel}} by {{WP|Ernest Hemmingway|Hemmingway}} or {{WP|For Whom the Bell Tolls (disambiguation)|one of the derived works}}.

==References==
{{Reflist}}

{{Tale of the Righteous}}
